title: Gifs only animate when video plays
author: madprops
body:
### Summary

I don't know if this is normal but the gifs in that are not being animated unless I play the video below. In Firefox for instance the gifs animate normally in a loop. At first I thought it was a network/load issue, but then I played the video and noticed that. That section is near the bottom of the page.

It happens on this site: https://merkoba.com/main.html

And here is the capture video:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b3e193c6-8219-401f-9353-7d5ae618e17b

### Operating system

Linux

### Steps to reproduce

I ran ladybird through `nix run nixpkgs#ladybird` on nixos.

And just went to https://merkoba.com/main.html

### Expected behavior

The gifs are supposed to run normally.

### Actual behavior

The gifs freeze until the video plays.

### URL for a reduced test case

https://merkoba.com/main.html

### HTML/SVG/etc. source for a reduced test case

```html
https://merkoba.com/main.html
```

### Log output and (if possible) backtrace


```shell
https://merkoba.com/main.html
```

### Screenshots or screen recordings

_No response_

### Build flags or config settings

_No response_

title: Windows: VERIFY(m_peer_pid != -1) crash in TransportSocketWindows::serialize_attachments when transferring a MessagePort
author: angrymuesli
body:
### Summary

On Windows, transferring a MessagePort (e.g. to a Worker via postMessage) crashes WebContent with `VERIFY(m_peer_pid != -1)` in `TransportSocketWindows::serialize_attachments`. Reproduces with a minimal test case (attached); breaks real sites such as proton.me login. Linux is unaffected.

### Operating system

Windows

### Steps to reproduce

1. On Windows, load the reduced test case below (or log in at proton.me)
2. WebContent crashes immediately on the `postMessage`

### Expected behavior

The port transfer succeeds, as it does on Linux — verified with a same-day master build (`4a669bfeff`) under WSL2 (Debian trixie), where both the test case and proton.me work.

### Actual behavior

WebContent crashes:

```
VERIFICATION FAILED: m_peer_pid != -1 at ..\..\Libraries\LibIPC\TransportSocketWindows.cpp:104
#0 IPC::TransportSocketWindows::serialize_attachments
#1 IPC::TransportSocketWindows::post_message
#2 IPC::MessageBuffer::transfer_message
#3 Web::HTML::MessagePort::send_message_on_transport
#4 Web::HTML::MessagePort::post_port_message
#5 Web::HTML::MessagePort::message_port_post_message_steps
#6 Web::HTML::MessagePort::post_message
```

The same VERIFY is reached both from page-side `postMessage` (trace above) and from `DedicatedWorkerGlobalScope::post_message` when a real site (proton.me) does the same; frames below `#6` differ and contain some nearest-symbol artifacts from a release build.

It looks like the MessagePort's transport never receives `set_peer_pid()` before the first attachment-bearing message, so handle duplication (OpenProcess/WSADuplicateSocketW) has no target PID. The Unix transport doesn't need a peer PID, which would explain why Linux is unaffected.

### URL for a reduced test case

N/A

### HTML/SVG/etc. source for a reduced test case

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<script>
  const worker = new Worker(URL.createObjectURL(
    new Blob(["onmessage = () => {};"], { type: "text/javascript" })));
  const channel = new MessageChannel();
  worker.postMessage("port", [channel.port2]);
</script>
```

### Log output and (if possible) backtrace


```shell
VERIFICATION FAILED: m_peer_pid != -1 at ..\..\Libraries\LibIPC\TransportSocketWindows.cpp:104
Stack trace (most recent call first):
#0  0x00007ff8fc1fb8cc in IPC::TransportSocketWindows::serialize_attachments
#1  0x00007ff8fc1fba8d in IPC::TransportSocketWindows::post_message

#3  0x00007ff8abb799a3 in Web::HTML::MessagePort::send_message_on_transport

#5  0x00007ff8abb791d6 in Web::HTML::MessagePort::message_port_post_message_steps

title: AppKit: Lambda return type deduction fails due to BOOL/bool mismatch in windowShouldClose:
author: Sm00shed

title: Compositor: CVDisplayLinkRelease called outside deprecated-declarations pragma block
author: Sm00shed
body:
**`CVDisplayLinkRelease` called outside deprecation suppression scope**

`CVDisplayLinkRelease` is deprecated since macOS 15.0. In `Services/Compositor/VSyncScheduler.cpp`, all other `CVDisplayLink*` calls in `DisplayLinkState::stop_display_link()` are wrapped in `#pragma clang diagnostic` suppression blocks, but `CVDisplayLinkRelease` on line 177 is called after the `pop`, leaving it outside the suppression scope.

Current code (~line 173):

```cpp
#    pragma clang diagnostic push
#    pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wdeprecated-declarations"
    CVDisplayLinkStop(display_link);
#    pragma clang diagnostic pop
    CVDisplayLinkRelease(display_link);
```

Fix:

```cpp
#    pragma clang diagnostic push
#    pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wdeprecated-declarations"
    CVDisplayLinkStop(display_link);
    CVDisplayLinkRelease(display_link);
#    pragma clang diagnostic pop
```

This results in a `-Wdeprecated-declarations` warning when building with Clang on macOS.

title: Transparency-related code sometimes reads garbage
author: BenWiederhake
body: ### Summary When I open futo.org with ladybird, the background around "What FUTO does differently" flickers with random colors along a "forward slash" diagonal line. This seems to be due to transparency-related code reading garbage data somewhere. Displaying a highly-transparent PNG and displaying something over it with "mix-blend-mode:overlay" triggers the bug. Note that this seems to depend on re-renders, so clicking and selecting stuff is necessary for an actual *flickering* to occur. I used my own tool ReproClipper to clip down the reproducing testcase until it was a manageable size: https://github.com/BenWiederhake/ReproClipper#repro_clipper Probably not related to 6640, 7254, and 9830, because this is about a bug that seems to cause random colors, not just black-white. Probably not related to 6922, because I don't see any console output. ### Operating system Linux ### Steps to reproduce 1. Open the reduced testcase below 2. Consider how evenly the background is colored 3. Click "Clickme!" many times (double-clicks are fine, too). Or resize the window. 4. Consider whether the background changes ### Expected behavior The background should be evenly grey. The background should not change when clicking "Clickme!" or resizing the window. ### Actual behavior The background contains a diagonal ("forward slash") line of glitchy artifacts of varying color and shape. These glitches move around and change when clicking. The angle of the diagonal line changes when resizing the window. ### URL for a reduced test case N/A ### HTML/SVG/etc. source for a reduced test case ```html ``` ### Log output and (if possible) backtrace ```shell None ``` ### Screenshots or screen recordings https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2c9ab5c2-b62c-446c-88c0-264fc020d4e4 ### Build flags or config settings default, 71c3b4fc974f9fadd05b4b8c5399cb62dfb97c53

title: Sticky element inside inline/unset elements
author: BenWiederhake
body:
### Summary When I open [GitHub Docs](https://docs.github.com/en/get-started/git-basics/about-remote-repositories) and scroll down, the navigation bar at the top moves out of the viewport, instead of being sticky on the screen. This seems to be because `position:sticky` is ignored if the parent element is `display:unset`. I used my own tool ReproClipper to clip down the reproducing testcase until it was a manageable size: https://github.com/BenWiederhake/ReproClipper#repro_clipper This is very different from #10623, because this issue is about an element that *should* stick to the screen, and 10623 is about an element that should *not* stick to the screen. Although 9345 uses similar terms, it is not related to this issue. ### Operating system Linux ### Steps to reproduce 1. Open the reduced testcase below 2. Find and track the "Header" text in the first line 3. Scroll down 4. Observe the new position of the "Header" line. ### Expected behavior "Header" should remain exactly where it is. ### Actual behavior "Header" scrolls up, as if it were not `position:sticky` at all. ### URL for a reduced test case N/A ### HTML/SVG/etc. source for a reduced test case ```html

title: Docs: BuildInstructionsLadybird.md doesn't mention the Qt ≥ 6.9 requirement introduced in 4aba06c3d8
author: angrymuesli
body:
### Summary

I pulled and built master on Friday without issues on my trixie WSL2 setup; after pulling again over the weekend, configure now fails since [4aba06c3d8](https://github.com/LadybirdBrowser/ladybird/commit/4aba06c3d8) ("UI/Qt: Require Qt version 6.9", 2026-07-10) landed:

```
CMake Error at Build/vcpkg/scripts/buildsystems/vcpkg.cmake:910 (_find_package):
  Could not find a configuration file for package "Qt6" that is compatible
  with requested version "6.9".

    /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/cmake/Qt6/Qt6Config.cmake, version: 6.8.2
```

`Documentation/BuildInstructionsLadybird.md` hasn't been updated to reflect this:

- The prerequisites line says "Qt6 development packages … are required" without a minimum version.
- The **Debian/Ubuntu** section instructs installing `qt6-base-dev` from the distro, but Debian 13 (trixie) ships Qt 6.8.2 and no current Ubuntu LTS ships 6.9 either, so following the instructions verbatim produces the error above. Qt 6.9+ is currently only available in Debian testing/sid and Ubuntu 25.10+.
- The **WSL2** section recommends "Ubuntu or Fedora" with the same package lists, so WSL users hit the same wall.

**Suggestion:** state the Qt ≥ 6.9 minimum in the prerequisites, and note which distro releases currently satisfy it (or point affected users at the vcpkg-provided Qt 6.10 mentioned in the commit message).

**Environment:** Debian 13 (trixie) under WSL2, master @ current

### Operating system

Linux

### Steps to reproduce

1. On Debian 13 (trixie) under WSL2, install the packages listed in the Debian/Ubuntu section of `Documentation/BuildInstructionsLadybird.md` (this installs `qt6-base-dev` 6.8.2, the newest available in trixie)
2. Clone master (any commit including or after 4aba06c3d8)
3. Run `./Meta/ladybird.py build`

### Expected behavior

Following the documented prerequisites for a listed distro produces a working configure/build, or the documentation states the minimum Qt version (≥ 6.9) so the reader knows their distro's Qt is too old before starting.

### Actual behavior

Configure fails because system Qt 6.8.2 doesn't satisfy the new `find_package(Qt6 6.9 REQUIRED)` in UI/Qt/CMakeLists.txt:

```
CMake Error at Build/vcpkg/scripts/buildsystems/vcpkg.cmake:910 (_find_package):
  Could not find a configuration file for package "Qt6" that is compatible
  with requested version "6.9".

    /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/cmake/Qt6/Qt6Config.cmake, version: 6.8.2
```

### URL for a reduced test case

N/A

### HTML/SVG/etc. source for a reduced test case

```html
N/A
```

### Log output and (if possible) backtrace


```shell
N/A — documentation issue, no crash involved
```

### Screenshots or screen recordings

_No response_

### Build flags or config settings

_No response_

title: CSS Animations break when multiple animations of same name are given
author: BenWiederhake
body:
### Summary

When I open futo.org with ladybird, the "Explore Open Tech" button doesn't get animated at all.

This seems to be because ladybird doesn't run animations at all if there's more than one of them, it seems? This isn't even a case of "technically not allowed, but other browsers do it", but it's in the spec: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/Properties/animation#formal_syntax ("Hash mark: the entity is repeated one or several times, each occurrence separated by a comma.")

I used my own tool ReproClipper to clip down the reproducing testcase until it was a manageable size: https://github.com/BenWiederhake/ReproClipper#repro_clipper

There doesn't seem to exist another issue like this, although this *might* end up being connected to #6549 or #2233.

### Operating system

Linux

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the reduced testcase below
2. Consider the angle of "HELLO", and how quickly it is rotating


### Expected behavior

"HELLO" should be rotating forever, at a constant speed.

### Actual behavior

"HELLO" is at a fixed angle.

### URL for a reduced test case

futo.org

### HTML/SVG/etc. source for a reduced test case

```html
<!DOCTYPE html><html><head><style>
#foo{width:99px;
  animation:q linear infinite 4s,q;
  animation-composition:add;}
@keyframes q{to{rotate:360deg}}
</style></head><body><div id="foo">HELLO
```

### Log output and (if possible) backtrace


```shell
None
```

### Screenshots or screen recordings

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/47a086b7-1cbb-486a-a64a-09c5c858e509

### Build flags or config settings

default, 71c3b4fc974f9fadd05b4b8c5399cb62dfb97c53

title: Text-align center sometimes re-positions a position-absolute child
author: BenWiederhake
body:
### Summary When I open a (local business) webpage with a particular wordpress gallery, the buttons and photos are completely misplaced. The cause seems to be that in ladybird, `text-align: center;` somehow interacts with a `position:absolute;` child, but only if `display:table;`. I don't understand. The `div{height:10px; border:solid blue 1px;}` line is purely for demonstration. The space between the two `div`s is relevant, for some reason? o.O I used my own tool ReproClipper to clip down the reproducing testcase until it was a manageable size: https://github.com/BenWiederhake/ReproClipper#repro_clipper I don't really know what to search for; GitHub shows too many hits, most of them seem irrelevant. #10309 is vaguely related, but seems like a separate issue. ### Operating system Linux ### Steps to reproduce 1. Open the reduced testcase below 2. Consider the location of the "XXXX" marker in relation to the window ### Expected behavior The "XXXXX" marker should be top-left by default. ### Actual behavior The "XXXXX" marker is horizontally center-aligned with its parent box, even though it is `position:absolute;`. ### URL for a reduced test case N/A ### HTML/SVG/etc. source for a reduced test case ```html
XXXXX ``` ### Log output and (if possible) backtrace ```shell None ``` ### Screenshots or screen recordings ### Build flags or config settings Default, 2a3bc6a32fdd35cf95536d4e80cb395dc2201fcd

title: SVG tspan causes letter spacing to fail
author: BenWiederhake
body: ### Summary When I open Sven's SudokuPad (a nice game website, e.g. https://sudokupad.app/kz9mq7ufo7 ) and enter a few numbers into a single cell (e.g. Ctrl-1 Ctrl-2 Ctrl-3 Ctrl-4), the cell content doesn't get displayed properly. The rot cause seems to be that `tspan`-elements within an SVG somehow get squished. My best guess is that the existence of `tspan` somehow prevents Ladybird to apply proper letterspacing. I used my own tool ReproClipper to clip down the reproducing testcase until it was a manageable size: https://github.com/BenWiederhake/ReproClipper#repro_clipper Github shows no hits so far using "svg tspan". Seems related to #6878, so @gmta. ### Operating system Linux ### Steps to reproduce 1. Open the enclosed testcase 2. Consider how the digits are arranged. (Sorry, I promise I'm trying my best to actually be descriptive, but this one really should be self-evident.) Alternatively, with the original Sudoku Pad page: 1. Open https://sudokupad.app/kz9mq7ufo7 2. Click "Start Puzzle" 3. Click a game cell to select it (it should then be highlighted by a lightblue border) 4. Hit Ctrl-1, Ctrl-2, Ctrl-3, Ctrl-4 on your keyboard to enter "candidates" into the cell. (Alternatively, use the menu to select "Centre"-mode, and click 1 2 3 4.) 5. Consider how the digits are arranged. ### Expected behavior The digits should be properly spaced and easily readable. ### Actual behavior The digits are smushed together and barely readable. ### URL for a reduced test case N/A ### HTML/SVG/etc. source for a reduced test case ```html ### Build flags or config settings default, 2a3bc6a32fdd35cf95536d4e80cb395dc2201fcd

title: Sticky-within-overflow shouldn't mean sticky on screen
author: BenWiederhake
body:
### Summary When I open Sven's SudokuPad (a nice game website, e.g. https://sudokupad.app/kz9mq7ufo7 ), the "Solve Counter" doesn't get displayed properly. The root cause seems to be that if an element is "sticky" within an "overflow-y:auto" container, then it shouldn't actually stick to the screen. I cannot fathom why *that* is the correct behavior, but it's a visual bug with actual impact, and both Firefox and Chrome behave different, so Ladybird should change something. I used my own tool ReproClipper to clip down the reproducing testcase until it was a manageable size: https://github.com/BenWiederhake/ReproClipper#repro_clipper This seems to be completely different from 58, 10367. Sound very similar to #4981, but that is apparently already fixed? Huh. ### Operating system Linux ### Steps to reproduce 1. Open the enclosed testcase 2. Resize the window so that not everything fits on the screen 3. Scroll around a bit ### Expected behavior The "Start Puzzle" text moves around when scrolling, i.e. it sticks to the other text. ### Actual behavior The "Start Puzzle" text does **NOT** move around when scrolling, i.e. it sticks to the screen. ### URL for a reduced test case N/A ### HTML/SVG/etc. source for a reduced test case ```html

F

F

F

F

F

Start Puzzle https://sudokupad.app/kz9mq7ufo7 ``` ### Log output and (if possible) backtrace ```shell None ``` ### Screenshots or screen recordings ### Build flags or config settings default, 2a3bc6a32fdd35cf95536d4e80cb395dc2201fcd

title: inset box-shadow with huge "spread" is ignored
author: BenWiederhake
body: ### Summary When I open a page on [Amnesty international](https://www.amnesty.de/glossar-fuer-diskriminierungssensible-sprache), the Telegram symbol (at the very bottom) is seemingly missing. This presumably affects many websites which "display" something by using this weird inverted-drawing method; Amnesty is just where I found it. The root cause seems to be that when the "spread" of an inset box-shadow is huge, the draw operation is dropped, instead of executing the draw as-is, or replacing it with a "fill everything" kind of operation. I wildly guess this is because ladybird detects that the spread is particularly huge, and "optimizes it away" in a wrong way. Also, when trying to experiment with https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/Properties/box-shadow , switching between the various previews, this always causes an infinite loop for me. This is probably a separate bug, but I'm not sure. ### Operating system Linux ### Steps to reproduce Amnesty website: 1. Open https://www.amnesty.de/glossar-fuer-diskriminierungssensible-sprache 2. Search for "Teile diesen Beitrag" 3. Consider the last three icons of the row: LinkedIn, WhatsApp, and Telegram Reduced testcase: 1. Open the reduced testcase (i.e. save to HTML-file and paste the address into the browser) 2. Consider the color of the background behind "x" ### Expected behavior Amnesty website: The last icon should be Telegram Reduced testcase: The background is a wide light-blue box ### Actual behavior Amnesty website: The Telegram icon is missing Reduced testcase: The background box is missing ### URL for a reduced test case N/A ### HTML/SVG/etc. source for a reduced test case ```html x ``` ### Log output and (if possible) backtrace ```shell None ``` ### Screenshots or screen recordings ### Build flags or config settings default, https://github.com/LadybirdBrowser/ladybird/commit/d0571bf476383ce248639496a50fe916f6e2b113

title: Font italicization doesn't work if not explicitly supported by font
author: BenWiederhake
body:
### Summary When I open a page on [Amnesty international](https://www.amnesty.de/glossar-fuer-diskriminierungssensible-sprache), the quotes sources (visible after clicking one of the sections) are rendered wrong. In particular, the text style(?) is regular, not italics. This presumably affects many websites, Amnesty is just where I found it. The root cause seems to be that Ladybird doesn't "create" the italics version of a font, if it isn't explicitly baked into the font data already. (This seems to include other attributes like font weight, i.e. "bold".) The reduced testcase contains the smallest CC-licensed font I could easily find. Since it's [CC-BY-4.0](https://tinyworlds.itch.io/free-pixel-font-thaleah), I expect that you will want to replace the specific font instance before adding this testcase to the test suite. (The bugtracker only find an unrelated issue for "italics" and nothing for "slanted".) ### Operating system Linux ### Steps to reproduce Amnesty website: 1. Open https://www.amnesty.de/glossar-fuer-diskriminierungssensible-sprache 2. Click the section titled "Ausländer" 3. Consider the text "Quelle: Neue Deutsche Medienmacher*innen Stand 06/2023" Reduced testcase: 1. Open the reduced testcase (i.e. save to HTML-file and paste the address into the browser) 2. Consider the text "C'est ne pas italics" ### Expected behavior "Quelle: Neue Deutsche Medienmacher*innen Stand 06/2023" and "C'est ne pas italics" **should** be in italics. (Also, "bold" **should** be bold.) ### Actual behavior "Quelle: Neue Deutsche Medienmacher*innen Stand 06/2023" and "C'est ne pas italics" **are not** in italics. (Also, "bold" **is not** bold.) ### URL for a reduced test case N/A ### HTML/SVG/etc. source for a reduced test case ```html C'est ne pas italics
C'est ne pas bold
C'est ordinaire ``` ### Log output and (if possible) backtrace ```shell None ``` ### Screenshots or screen recordings ### Build flags or config settings default, d0571bf476383ce248639496a50fe916f6e2b113 (i.e. after https://github.com/LadybirdBrowser/ladybird/pull/10579 has been merged)

title: Ladybird fails to launch on macOS 27 Developer Beta 3 with SQL runtime error
author: vinodhn
body:
### Summary

When running ladybird on commit `ba34ea3dae`, it crashes upon launch with an SQL Logic Error. This is similar to issue #10379 without the sandbox error. I am on the current latest macOS 27 Golden Gate developer beta, beta 3.

However, when running with the `--disable-sql-database` argument, Ladybird launches as expected. Upon quitting after launching with this option, there is a message about the ipc/rcv port changing but no SQL error. 

### Operating system

macOS

### Steps to reproduce

1. Clone ladybird
2. Build and run with `./Meta/ladybird.py run`


### Expected behavior

Ladybird launching to about:newtab

### Actual behavior

Ladybird fails to launch

### URL for a reduced test case

N/A

### HTML/SVG/etc. source for a reduced test case

```html
N/A
```

### Log output and (if possible) backtrace


```shell
347159.478 Ladybird(98035) MachBootstrapLi(2103459): mach_msg failed: (ipc/rcv) port changed
Runtime error: SQL logic error
```

### Screenshots or screen recordings

_No response_

### Build flags or config settings

_No response_

title: Sandbox violation: disallowed syscall access (21) on Arch Linux (Wayland), browser starts with --disable-sandbox
author: cricarro
body:
### Summary

Launching Ladybird normally fails during startup with a sandbox violation.


### Operating system

Linux

### Steps to reproduce

### Steps to reproduce

1. Install the latest `ladybird-git` package on Arch Linux.
2. Ensure the system has a working Vulkan installation (the issue still occurs after installing `vulkan-intel`).
3. Start Ladybird normally:

   ```
   Ladybird
   ```
4. Observe that the browser fails to start with:

   ```
   Sandbox violation: disallowed syscall access (21)
   Failed to receive message_id: 8
   VERIFICATION FAILED: !is_error()
   ```
5. Start Ladybird with:

   ```
   Ladybird --disable-sandbox
   ```
6. Observe that the browser starts successfully.


### Expected behavior

When started normally, Ladybird should initialize successfully with the sandbox enabled and open the browser window without crashing.



### Actual behavior

Ladybird fails during startup with the following messages:

```
Sandbox violation: disallowed syscall access (21)
Failed to receive message_id: 8
VERIFICATION FAILED: !is_error()
Illegal instruction (core dumped)
```

The browser does not open when started normally.

Running Ladybird with `--disable-sandbox` starts the browser successfully, indicating that the failure is related to the sandbox.


### URL for a reduced test case

N/A - The crash occurs during browser startup before any page is loaded.

### HTML/SVG/etc. source for a reduced test case

```html
N/A - The crash occurs during browser startup before any page is loaded.
```

### Log output and (if possible) backtrace


```shell
$ Ladybird

Ladybird PID file '/run/user/1000/Ladybird.pid' exists with PID 37176, but process cannot be found
Sandbox violation: disallowed syscall access (21), architecture 0xc000003e, instruction pointer 0x7fec61919c5d, args [0x7fec57874920, 0x4, 0x7fec5787493d, 0x7fec5787499e, 0x1, 0x5c3d5740000]
11985.748 Ladybird(37176): Failed to receive message_id: 8
VERIFICATION FAILED: !is_error() at /home/cristiano/.cache/yay/ladybird-git/src/ladybird/AK/Error.h:171
Stack trace (most recent call first):
#0  0x000055649397e8cb at /opt/ladybird/usr/bin/Ladybird
#1  0x00005564904d1175 at /opt/ladybird/usr/bin/Ladybird
#2  0x00005564904b3afe at /opt/ladybird/usr/bin/Ladybird
#3  0x0000556490469f3a at /opt/ladybird/usr/bin/Ladybird
#4  0x0000556490474b36 at /opt/ladybird/usr/bin/Ladybird
#5  0x000055649045072d at /opt/ladybird/usr/bin/Ladybird
#6  0x000055649044ba58 at /opt/ladybird/usr/bin/Ladybird
#7  0x0000556490c42f50 in main at /opt/ladybird/usr/bin/Ladybird
#8  0x00007ff90b627740 at /usr/lib/libc.so.6
#9  0x00007ff90b627878 in __libc_start_main at /usr/lib/libc.so.6
#10 0x000055649043eee4 at /opt/ladybird/usr/bin/Ladybird
Illegal instruction (core dumped)

Additional information:

* Vulkan is working correctly (`vulkaninfo --summary` succeeds).
* The browser starts successfully when launched with `Ladybird --disable-sandbox`.
* The issue only occurs when the sandbox is enabled.
```

### Screenshots or screen recordings

_No response_

### Build flags or config settings

_No response_

title: /Meta/ladybird.py: add cross compile option
author: peppergrayxyz
body:
Would be nice to have cross compiling out of the box, something like
`/Meta/ladybird.py $command --target=$CHOST --sysroot=$ROOTFS`

title: FEATURE REQUEST: keyboard first or fully customizable keybindings
author: pedro757
body:
Something I hate about chromium and other current browser is how opinionated they are about keybindings, now allowing me to change defaults. Current browsers are not keyboard first.

I am a VIM user so I want to be able to customize the browser experience fully.

title: Text is slighly blurry
author: ilyapopov
body: ### Summary Test is lightly blurrier in Ladybird when compared to Firefox and Chrome. This is on Linux with Wayland and AMD RADV drivers (Mesa 26.0.3) This occurs both with and without fractional scaling.
